rows.locks: Planted four-gates failure attributes to m68-real-title-run/#2025 and produces evidence-linked root-cause hypotheses from verdict, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, and loop-coverage attribution.
Diagnosis basedOnRefs include verdict.json, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, loop-coverage attribution, source-apply, and trust-gradient; no parallel verifier or store is introduced.
Fix proposals are patch-preview.v1 source-apply artifacts scoped to Rust engine/source-apply targets under crates/ with source freshness and rollback metadata.
High-risk/source-affecting proposals remain blocked/unapplied, forbid auto_apply/merge, and require the thin human go/no-go tail through trust-gradient.
The diagnosis to proposal demo runs the autonomous path with no human input and records no source mutation, no high-risk auto-apply, and no self-apply authority.
Hidden human input, high-risk auto-apply ledger drift, non-source targets, and missing human go/no-go review drift fail closed in Rust tests.

Coverage v62 is test-only and preserves openchrome, scenario verdicts, the four gates plus design-integrity, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, loop-coverage attribution, evolve, source-apply, and trust-gradient boundaries.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v62 is test-only Rust regression coverage over the existing openchrome/evidence pipeline: scenario verdicts, the four gates plus design-integrity, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, loop-coverage attribution, evolve, source-apply, and trust-gradient. It introduces no new verification engine, no new data plane, and no new persistent store. The autonomous path completes with zero human input; high-risk/source-affecting changes are never auto-applied and remain blocked for a thin human go/no-go. Fun/taste and release go/no-go remain human Ring 2. #1 and #23 remain open.
